scope: Vim modal editing for the TUI composer

requiredBehaviors: normal and insert mode state factories
hjkl, word, WORD, line, file, find, repeat-find, and g-prefixed motions
operator-pending transitions for motions, text objects, find, repeat-find, and g-prefix
edgeCases: zero is a line-start motion rather than a count prefix
count values clamp to the maximum vim count
replace with an empty literal cancels instead of deleting
n and N are bounded to stored find-repeat semantics

requiredBehaviors: linewise operators, paste, indent, replace, x, join, and open-line operations
word, WORD, quote, bracket, brace, paragraph, and tag text objects
edgeCases: linewise deletes keep newline-backed register content
self-closing and unmatched tags return no text object
nested same-name tags select the innermost balanced pair
paragraph around objects include the adjacent blank-line separator

requiredBehaviors: tui.vimMode enables the VimTextInput composer path
legacy editorMode remains a fallback when the nested flag is absent
StatusLine renders insert and normal vim mode indicators when the custom status line is active
PromptInputFooterLeftSide renders insert and normal vim mode indicators when the custom status line is absent
processTextPrompt can finalize vim routing keys before prompt message construction
processUserInput can apply vim finalization before bash, slash, or prompt dispatch
edgeCases: tui.vimMode false overrides legacy editorMode vim
settings writes mirror tui.vimMode and editorMode
ConfigTool dotted global settings preserve sibling fields
vim routing finalization leaves bash and slash dispatch unchanged when disabled

equivalenceNotes: The pinned donor vim files were already present before OC-06; this item finishes the destination filename, config, composer, tests, and checklist-required extensions.
n and N intentionally repeat f/F/t/T find memory because the TUI composer does not own slash-search state.
StatusLine.tsx owns visible vim mode status while a custom status line is active; PromptInputFooterLeftSide owns the built-in prompt footer indicator otherwise.
PromptInput owns live composer modal transitions; processTextPrompt/processUserInput expose route-level vim finalization for dispatch surfaces that receive unfinalized routing keys.
